Users

The Users page lets administrators create and manage user accounts, set passwords, and assign roles. A user must have the access-panel permission (via their role) to log in and use the application.

Managing users

  1. Go to Settings › Users.
  2. Click New User to create a user, or click the edit icon on an existing user to modify them.
  3. Fill in the user's name, email, and password. Select a role.
  4. Save.

Users can be deleted from the same page. Deletion is permanent and requires confirmation.

User fields

FieldRequiredNotes
NameYesDisplay name; max 255 characters
EmailYesMust be a valid, unique email address; max 255 characters. Used as the login username.
PasswordYes (on create)Minimum 8 characters; must match the confirmation field. When editing an existing user, leave blank to keep their current password.
Confirm passwordYes (when setting a password)Must match the password field exactly
RoleNoThe role that determines what this user can do. A user with no role has no permissions and can only log in if their role includes access-panel — without a role, they will not be able to access any page.

Assigning roles

Select a role from the dropdown when creating or editing a user. A user holds one role at a time. The permissions attached to that role determine everything the user can see and do in the application. To change what a user can do, either change their role or edit the permissions on their current role at Settings › Roles. See Roles & Permissions for the full permission reference.

Panel access

The access-panel permission controls whether a user can reach the application at all after logging in. A user who has successfully logged in but whose role does not include access-panel will be blocked from all pages. Make sure every role intended for active users includes access-panel. The Administrator and accountant roles both include this permission by default.

Permissions

  • users-view-any — to view the user list
  • users-view — to view a single user's details
  • users-create — to create new users
  • users-update — to edit existing users
  • users-delete — to delete users
